sections.paragraph:
to remove, or to be removed, unused in Kal. (Nearly connected is the Syr. ܗܰܠܗܶܠ to put far, to remove, and Arab. هَلَّ II. to stay away, to go away, to recede.)

[In Thes. this is not given as a verb; the Niphal is made denom. from הָלְאָה.]

Niphal, partic. הַנַּהֲלָאָה removed, far off, collectively, the far removed, the remote, Mic 4:7. Hence-
